selected_goal: Add a strict companion-input mode to asset-reference validation so release-style checks can hard-fail when persisted manifest/export sidecars are missing.
why_this_now: Iteration 2 repaired the default live gate by warning on absent companion outputs, but its audit identified a release-risk: ok=true can be misread as release-ready even when persisted asset_manifest and downstream sidecars are absent. A bounded strict mode keeps local validation usable while preserving a hard gate for workflows that require generated companions.
repo_evidence: .agent-runs/2026-06-18T22-15-18-167Z/iteration-02/04-audit-report.json lists permissive missing companion outputs as the main regression risk.
scripts/validate_asset_references.py exposes the validator as a project gate but has no strictness control.
src/exam_bank/asset_manifest.py already centralizes missing-input handling, so this can be implemented without touching output generation or canonical assets.
acceptance_criteria: The default validator behavior from iteration 2 remains ok with warnings when companion inputs are absent.
A strict companion-input option makes absent asset manifest, Asterion exports, Content Lab export, and topic-routing sidecar hard validation errors.
The CLI exposes the strict mode without changing default command behavior.
No generated output files are written.
test_expectations: Add a test that calls validate_asset_references with strict companion inputs and asserts absent companion files produce missing_validation_input errors and ok=false.
Keep the iteration-2 default warning test passing.
Run the asset-manifest test file and both default and strict CLI commands against the current workspace.
implementation_boundaries: Do not generate or delete output files.
Do not change the meaning of malformed present companion files.
Do not change extraction, export generation, or image rendering behavior.
repo_hygiene_risk: Low. The change is a parameter and CLI flag, but it must avoid broad command semantics churn.
rollback_plan: Remove the strict parameter, CLI flag, and strict-mode test.
done_when: The new strict-mode test fails before implementation and passes after implementation.
Default scripts/validate_asset_references.py still exits zero on the current workspace.
Strict scripts/validate_asset_references.py --strict-companion-inputs exits non-zero on the current workspace with missing_validation_input errors.
